name: arch_inv_unsatisfiable
code:
proof fn phi_1_arch_inv_unsatisfiable(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If Arch::inv is unsatisfiable, every spec depending on it is vacuously true and provides no guarantees

name: entry_base_equals_next_entry_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_equals_next_entry_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        idx < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx) == arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: If entry_base equals next_entry_base, entries have zero width and address regions collapse to empty intervals

name: arch_inv_forces_single_layer
code:
proof fn phi_3_arch_inv_forces_single_layer(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() > 1,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If multi-layer architectures are impossible under inv, the hierarchical page table structure is unmodelable

name: directory_inv_forbids_subdirectories
code:
proof fn phi_4_directory_inv_forbids_subdirectories(dir: Directory)
    requires
        dir.inv(),
        dir.entries.len() > 0,
    ensures
        forall|i: nat| i < dir.entries.len() ==> !(#[trigger] dir.entries[i as int] is Directory),
{
}
reason: If inv forbids all subdirectories, the page table cannot represent a multi-level hierarchy

name: well_formed_forces_single_entry
code:
proof fn phi_5_well_formed_forces_single_entry(dir: Directory)
    requires
        dir.well_formed(),
    ensures
        dir.num_entries() <= 1,
{
}
reason: If well_formed forces at most one entry, directories cannot map the full address space with 512-entry page tables
